definition: The paid Elementor Pro plugin that extends the core editor with advanced features.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the paid plan or features included in the Pro offering.

examples.correct: Upgrade to Editor Pro to access custom CSS and dynamic content.

notes: Always capitalize P in Pro
Never invert word order

definition: A web property created and managed by a user using Elementor.

usage.when_to_use: In product UI, onboarding, settings, and instructional copy.
usage.when_not_to_use: Avoid 'website' in product UI; acceptable in marketing copy only if decided.

examples.correct: Choose a name for your site.
examples.avoid: Choose a name for your website.

notes: Preferred for brevity and consistency
'Website' may be acceptable in marketing copy only

avoid: editor (when referring to the ecosystem or product family)
definition: A general term describing Elementor as a platform for building websites.

usage.when_to_use: When talking about Elementor at a high level or across multiple products.

examples.correct: Elementor is a website builder for creators.

notes: Builder = ecosystem
Editor = product

avoid: builder (when referring to the specific product experience)
definition: The specific Elementor product used to design and edit WordPress sites.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the in-product editing experience.

examples.correct: The Elementor Editor gives you full visual control.

notes: Builder = ecosystem
Editor = product

definition: A named tier that defines features, limits, and entitlements.

usage.when_to_use: To describe what a user is on or can upgrade to; when comparing tiers or capabilities; in pricing, upgrades, and eligibility messaging.
usage.when_not_to_use: When referring to billing state or renewal mechanics.

examples.correct: Your current plan includes custom fonts and dynamic content.

notes: Plans define what you get, not how you pay
Never use membership (no exceptions)

definition: The ongoing billing relationship between the user and Elementor.

usage.when_to_use: Payments, renewals, invoices, cancellations; billing settings and legal copy.

examples.correct: Your subscription renews on May 12.

notes: Subscription = how you pay
Plan = what you get

definition: Banned term. Imprecise, community-oriented, and inconsistent with product and billing models.

notes: BANNED — never use
Replace with 'plan' or 'subscription' based on context

definition: The open-source content management system Elementor is built for.

usage.when_to_use: Any reference to the platform or ecosystem.

examples.correct: Elementor is the leading website builder for WordPress.

notes: Capital W, capital P
Never sentence-case or lowercase

definition: A UI element that appears over page content to display messages or collect input.

usage.when_to_use: Product UI, features, settings, documentation.

examples.correct: Create a pop-up to capture leads.
examples.avoid: Create a popup for email signup.

notes: Lowercase p
Hyphenated
Consistent with modern UI terminology

definition: Elementor's most comprehensive and expensive offering, including the complete website-building experience with all Elementor capabilities.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the all-inclusive Elementor offering; in pricing, upgrades, comparisons, and plan eligibility.
usage.when_not_to_use: As a generic adjective (e.g. 'one solution'); lowercased or styled differently.

examples.correct: Upgrade to One for the complete website-building experience.
examples.avoid: Upgrade to one for everything Elementor offers.

notes: Both 'One' and 'Elementor One' are approved — use 'One' in product UI and short-form contexts, 'Elementor One' in full product names and external communications
Always capitalized — never 'one' or 'ONE'
Treated as a proper product name, not a number
Avoid adding unnecessary suffixes unless defined elsewhere (e.g. 'One plan')

definition: Describes a bundled experience that includes multiple tools or capabilities in a single offering.

usage.when_to_use: To describe One conceptually; in explanatory or marketing-leaning product copy.

examples.correct: One is an all-in-one solution for building websites.
examples.avoid: One is an all in one solution.

notes: Always hyphenated
Lowercase
Descriptor, not a product name

definition: The technology used to generate, improve, or automate outputs (e.g., text, design, suggestions) in Elementor experiences.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the technology itself in any audience or context (product UI, blogs, website, email).
usage.when_not_to_use: Don't spell out 'artificial intelligence' (even on first mention).

examples.correct: Use AI to generate copy for your site.
examples.avoid: Use Artificial Intelligence to generate copy for your site.

notes: Always uppercase: AI
Treat as a standard acronym, not a branded name
Lead with user goal, not the technology itself
Preferred framing: emphasize outcomes (faster, better) rather than the technology

definition: An add-on that extends functionality in WordPress or Elementor.

usage.when_to_use: In product UI, documentation, and technical content; when referring to extensions installed in WordPress or Elementor.

examples.correct: Install the plugin to start building your site.
examples.avoid: Install the plug-in to start building your site.

notes: Use lowercase 'plugin' in running text
Capitalize 'Plugin' only at the start of a sentence or as part of a proper name (e.g. Elementor Pro plugin)
Never hyphenate

definition: A website that contains a single page, where all content is organized into sections and accessed by scrolling or in-page navigation.

usage.when_to_use: The site has a simple structure or a focused story (e.g., portfolio, event, or landing page).
notes: Avoid confusing with: landing page (purpose) or single page template (Theme Builder concept)

definition: A website made up of multiple pages, each with its own URL (e.g., Home, About, Contact).

usage.when_to_use: The site has more content, multiple topics, or needs clear navigation between pages.
notes: Structure is divided across pages, not sections

definition: A standalone page designed around a single goal, such as capturing leads or driving conversions.

usage.when_to_use: Referring to purpose or intent, not structure.
notes: A landing page can exist within a multi-page site or as a one-page site

definition: The process of moving a site and its subscription ownership to the client, while the agency remains involved and continues managing the site.

notes: The client becomes the subscription owner, but the agency keeps access and maintains the ongoing relationship

definition: Creates a new subscription for a client to purchase through a dedicated checkout link generated by the agency.

notes: The client becomes the subscription owner after checkout, while the agency continues to manage and work on the site

definition: A structured program status assigned to an agency based on defined eligibility and performance criteria. Each tier grants access to a defined set of benefits and commercial terms.

notes: Used in Partner Program context

definition: An agency is a service provider that builds and manages websites for clients. A Partner is an agency that has been accepted into the Partner Program and participates in its tier structure, benefits, and commercial terms.

notes: Use 'Agency' for general service providers
Use 'Partner' specifically for those in the Partner Program

definition: A centralized dashboard for monitoring, maintaining, and optimizing WordPress sites.

usage.when_to_use: Referring to the standalone product or capability family.

examples.correct: Connect your sites to Manage. Open Manage. Manage dashboard.

notes: Product name — always capitalized
Used in marketing, documentation, and cross-product references
Avoid overusing inside One UI surfaces
Use 'Site management' (sentence case) inside Elementor One or WordPress admin surfaces

definition: The Site management experience within Elementor One that enables monitoring, maintenance, and optimization actions on a site.

usage.when_to_use: Inside Elementor One or WordPress admin surfaces.

examples.correct: Open Site management. Site management actions are paused.

notes: Written in sentence case
Describes the experience, not the product brand
Preferred in: WordPress admin, One dashboard, alerts, navigation, operational UI copy

definition: Elementor's agentic AI that performs actions and creates assets inside WordPress.

examples.correct: Ask Angie

definition: A reusable UI element added to a page.

examples.correct: Rename widget

definition: A widget generated by Angie based on a user request.

examples.correct: Create custom widget

definition: A safe environment where Angie's changes don't affect the live site.

examples.correct: Enter Test mode

definition: Makes changes from Test Mode visible on your live site.

examples.correct: Publish to live

definition: The public-facing version of the website visitors see.

examples.correct: Affect your live site

definition: Angie's proposed implementation before executing a request.

examples.correct: Review build plan

definition: An instruction given to Angie describing what to create or change.

examples.correct: Send request to Angie

definition: Angie attempts to automatically diagnose and repair an issue.

examples.correct: Fix with Angie

definition: A reusable piece of code that extends or customizes behavior in WordPress or on a site.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to reusable code-based assets in the Community library; when describing functionality that goes beyond standard Widgets or templates.
usage.when_not_to_use: When referring to visual elements (use Widget or template instead); when referring broadly to all community assets.

examples.correct: Add a snippet to customize your dashboard behavior.
examples.avoid: Add this widget to customize your dashboard behavior.

notes: Snippets are code-based and differ from Widgets (UI elements) and templates (layout structures)
Use 'snippet' alone in UI unless technical clarification is required

definition: A centralized library of artifacts created with Angie, saved to your account and available across sites.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to where Angie-generated artifacts are stored and managed; in flows related to saving, browsing, reusing, or managing generated items.
usage.when_not_to_use: When referring to items stored only within a specific site; as a generic term for any media or WordPress library.

examples.correct: Save this widget to your Cloud Library to reuse it on other sites.
examples.avoid: Save this widget to your library.

notes: Always capitalized as 'Cloud Library'
Refers to cross-site storage, not site-level storage

definition: Reusable items created with Angie, such as widgets, sections, and site parts, that can be saved and used across sites.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to outputs generated by Angie; in flows related to saving, reusing, or managing generated items.
usage.when_not_to_use: When referring to standard Elementor elements created manually.

examples.correct: Save this artifact to your Cloud Library to reuse it later.
examples.avoid: Save this asset to reuse it later.

notes: Always capitalized as 'Artifacts' when used as a system term
Refers specifically to Angie-generated outputs

definition: Elementor's next-generation editing experience, built on a modern CSS-based architecture. Replaces the traditional Section → Column → Widget structure with div-based layout, native Flexbox positioning, atomic components, and design variables.

usage.when_to_use: In user-facing and internal communication when referring to the new editor.
notes: Avoid version-based naming
When clarification is needed: 'Atomic editor (the new Elementor editor)'

avoid: Global blocks (unless referring to legacy feature)
definition: Reusable elements that stay synced wherever they appear across your site. Edit a Component once, and the changes automatically update in every instance.

notes: Use for repeating sections such as headers, cards, CTAs, or layouts
Short definition: 'A reusable element that updates everywhere at once'

avoid: CSS classes (unless referring to code specifically)
definition: Reusable style collections that can be applied across multiple elements. Update a Class once, and the changes automatically sync everywhere the Class is used.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to reusable styles in the Atomic Editor.
notes: Classes can control layout, spacing, typography, colors, effects, and responsive behavior
Multiple Classes can be applied to the same element
Short definition: 'A reusable set of styles you can apply across your site'
Based on Elementor's V4 CSS-first styling system

avoid: Global colors (unless referring to legacy V3 feature)
Global fonts (unless referring to legacy V3 feature)
definition: Reusable design values used across your site, such as colors, typography, spacing, and sizes. Update a Variable once, and every connected element updates automatically.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to shared design values like brand colors, font sizes, spacing, and dimensions.
notes: Variables can be used inside Classes and individual element styles
Short definition: 'A reusable design value that updates everywhere it's used'
Based on Elementor's V4 design system workflow

definition: The Style tab contains the visual styling controls shared across Atomic Elements. Provides a consistent interface for managing layout, spacing, typography, backgrounds, borders, effects, responsive styles, and Classes.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the styling workspace in the Atomic Editor.
notes: Shared across Atomic Elements for a consistent editing experience
Element-specific settings appear in the General tab
Responsive styling can be adjusted directly from the Style tab
Short definition: 'The centralized styling workspace for Atomic Elements'

definition: Uptime monitoring checks whether a site is available and helps users review downtime incidents in one place.

usage.when_to_use: Use 'Uptime monitoring' when introducing or describing the feature. Use 'Uptime' as the section or page name when context already makes it clear this is about site availability. Use 'Monitoring' when referring to the active monitoring state or action, especially in status messages and controls.

examples.correct: Uptime | New uptime monitoring | Monitoring active | Pause monitor | Monitor your site's availability and review downtime incidents in one place.

notes: Uptime monitoring is the clearest feature name for explanatory copy
Uptime works well for navigation and page titles because it's short and scannable
Monitoring should usually appear with context (e.g. 'Monitoring active') so it doesn't feel too broad
Use 'downtime' for periods when the site was unavailable
Keep incident copy factual and operational, not alarming
